name: Older-adult CDI FMT case review supports disease-context safety but not aging efficacy
summary: Older-adult CDI experience supports feasibility and disease-context effectiveness in older recipients while bounding aging interpretation.
statement: In a case review of adults aged 65 or older treated with FMT for Clostridium difficile infection, CDI resolved in 27 of 31 respondents; serious adverse events and deaths occurred during follow-up but were not attributed to FMT, and the most common reported adverse event was subjective worsening of arthritis in 4 of 31 respondents.
population_or_model: Adults aged 65 years or older with Clostridium difficile infection
time_horizon: Clinical follow-up after FMT for CDI
quantitative_note: CDI resolution was reported in 27 of 31 respondents, and subjective worsening of arthritis in 4 of 31 respondents.
caveats: This is recurrent or refractory infection treatment, not aging, frailty, or healthy-aging intervention evidence.
Case-review safety does not substitute for controlled chronic-use safety in older adults.
Disease eradication should not be counted as ecosystem-replacement benefit for non-CDI aging populations.
